description_long: This object is an elongated, conical stone with a smooth, glossy surface and a distinctive network of brown, zigzagging lines over a white base. The pattern resembles the intricate markings found on certain marine shells, such as those of the Conus genus. The stone tapers to a rounded point at one end and has a slightly wider, blunter base at the other, with a subtle indentation visible on the base. Its surface appears highly polished, reflecting light and giving it a sleek, almost ceramic-like appearance. The coloration and patterning are consistent across all sides, with the brown lines forming irregular, web-like shapes. There are no visible cracks, chips, or signs of significant weathering, suggesting it has been well-preserved or recently polished.
description: A smooth, glossy, conical stone with intricate brown zigzag patterns on a white background, resembling a polished marine shell.
description_view_0: Side view showing the elongated, conical shape and the full extent of the brown zigzag pattern on the white surface.
description_view_1: View from the blunter base, highlighting the rounded end and the dense concentration of patterning near the tip.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, displaying the smooth, continuous surface and the uninterrupted pattern along the length.
description_view_3: View from the pointed end, emphasizing the tapering shape and the subtle indentation at the base.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or accent stone on the ground
attribute_pattern: Intricate, irregular brown zigzag and web-like lines over a white background, forming a natural, shell-like pattern.
attribute_weathering: Smooth and highly polished, with no visible signs of weathering or surface aging.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts sharply with natural stones and man-made structures due to its unique pattern and glossy finish; often used as a decorative accent.
